zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Hart於1337年出生在英國肯特法維沙安時，她的父親Robert Hart25歲，母親Mrs. Robert Hart26歲。她於1350年與Sir John de Northwode在英國英格蘭諾福爾可阿申維爾索普結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1398年9月在她的家鄉享年61歲。

zh-hans.Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England: 英国諾福爾可阿申維爾索普
zh-hans.Isle of Sheppey, Kent, England: 英国肯特易斯爾 本 謝坡
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Hart于1337年出生在英国肯特法維沙安时，她的父亲Robert Hart25岁，母亲Mrs. Robert Hart26岁。她于1350年与Sir John de Northwode在英国英格兰諾福爾可阿申維爾索普结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1398年9月在她的家乡享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and North German: nickname from Middle English hert (Old English heorot), Middle Low German hërte, harte ‘hart, stag’, perhaps for a quick-footed or timorous individual.
German: variant of   Hardt   1 and 2. It is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name or nickname from German and Yiddish hart ‘hard’.

variants.name: Hartmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Heinz, Helmut, Horst, Klaus, Manfred, Dieter, Dietrich, Franz.
North German and Danish: variant of   Hart   1.

German and Swiss German: from an ancient Germanic personal name composed of the elements hard ‘hard, strong’ + man ‘man’. This surname is also found in the Netherlands, France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Czechia, and some other European countries; see also below. Compare Hartman .

variants.name: Francoeur
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Jacques, Aime, Marcel, Normand, Alcide, Amie, Anselme, Cecile, Clemence, Dominique, Georges.
French:
soldier's name from franc coeur ‘sincere heart’.

variants.name: Hartin
meanings: Irish (Donegal): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hArtáin ‘descendant of Artán’, a personal name formed from a diminutive of Art, a byname meaning ‘bear’ or ‘hero’. Compare Hart 8.
English: habitational name from Harting (Sussex), from the Old English personal name Heort + the Old English groupname suffix -ingas.

variants.name: Hartel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Erna, Gunter, Kurt, Lutz, Otto.
German (also Härtel): from a pet form of an ancient Germanic compound personal name based on the element hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’.
French (Normandy): occupational name, a derivative of   Hart   7.

variants.name: De Hart
meanings: Dutch: from Middle Dutch hart, modern Dutch hert ‘deer, hart’ or hart ‘heart’ (see Hart 5), with the definite article de ‘the’.

variants.name: Hardt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Ernst, Erwin, Frieda, Gerhard, Gottlieb, Gunther, Hans, Heinz, Reinhold, Udo.
German: topographic name for someone who lived by woods used as pasture, from Middle High German hart.
German: from a short form of an ancient Germanic compound personal name beginning with hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’.

variants.name: Harten
meanings: North German: patronymic from   Hart   1.
Dutch: patronymic from   Hart   4.
Dutch (Van Harten): habitational name from a place so named near Renkum, Gelderland.

variants.name: Hartt
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Hart  .

variants.name: Harte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Sinead.
English and North German: variant of   Hart   1.
Dutch: variant of   Hart   4.

variants.name: Hartjes
meanings: Dutch: patronymic from a pet form of   Hart   4.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Hart was born in 1337, in Faversham, Kent, England, her father, Robert Hart, was 25 and her mother, Mrs. Robert Hart, was 26. She married Sir John de Northwode in 1350, in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died in September 1398, in her hometown, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Hart est née en 1337, à Faversham, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Robert Hart, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Robert Hart, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John de Northwode en 1350, à Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en septembre 1398, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Hart 1337 in Faversham, Kent, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert Hart 25 und ihre Mutter Mrs. Robert Hart 26. Sie hat 1350 in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom Sir John de Northwode geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb im September 1398 im Alter von 61 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Hart è nata il nel 1337 nel Faversham, Kent, England, sua padre, Robert Hart, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Mrs. Robert Hart, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Sir John de Northwode nel 1350, nel Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel settembre 1398, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 61 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Hartが1337年にイギリスケントファバーシャムで生まれた時，父Robert Hartは25歳，母Mrs. Robert Hartは26歳であった。1350年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクアシュウエルソルペでSir John de Northwodeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1398年9月に故郷で61歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Hart(이)가 1337년 영국 켄트 파버샤에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Hart(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. Robert Hart(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1350년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 애슈웰소에서 Sir John de Northwode(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1398년 9월 그녀의 고향에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Hart nasceu em 1337, em Faversham, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert Hart, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Robert Hart, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John de Northwode em 1350, em Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em setembro de 1398, em sua cidade natal, com 61 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Hart родилась в 1337 г. в местности фавиршам, кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert Hart было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Robert Hart, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John de Northwode в 1350 г. в местности ашвеллтаурп, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла сентябрь 1398 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 61 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Hart nació en 1337, en Faversham, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert Hart, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Mrs. Robert Hart, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Sir John de Northwode en 1350, en Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en septiembre de 1398, en su pueblo, a la edad de 61 años.
